title: 20s Q preview and its connections to Shin
display_title: 20s Q preview and its connections to Shin
wikitext:
[]: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by Pluto » Mon Jul 12, 2021 8:57 pm This is a summary from eyes only’s video who attended the stage greeting and summarized the information that was cleared up regarding the preview to Q in 2.0 as well as some other details regarding the Q preview. Stage Greeting On July 11, a stage greeting was held and Anno talked about the 14 year timeskip. Gendo and Fuyutuski were removed from power and Kaoru and Kaji became commander and vice commander at this time. User Nuclear Lunchbox also translated some tweets in this topic that corroborate this. He thinks the scene in the Q preview that corresponds to when they are deposed is shown above. Q Preview In the image about where the children of fate assemble, there are 4 shadows with Kaoru. We don’t know for sure who these shadows are but in the Image Contents for the preview in “ha” it’s written that Kaoru is surrounded by 4 Rei’s (3 Rei’s between the ages of 3-10 and a fully grown Rei). We see a corollary image later on in the preview. So Kaoru is surrounded by 4 Rei’s in the “magic square”. There’s some strange writing in it. Adam’s Motif We know that the symbols in the magic circle correspond to Ultraman characters. As stated in another topic, there’s an ultraman episode where the ultraman team goes into the minus space and are captured crucified on planet Golgotha. Then, Ultraman ace goes to help them. Of course in Shin, the minus space is where the Golgotha object resides. Gendo says the minus space is where they found the Adams and six spears. There’s also a connection to where in this ultraman episode, the 4 crucified ultras give their power to Ace to gain a new ability called “Space Q” to defeat the enemy. This is reminiscent when Eva 08 absorbs the 4 opfer types to gain the plus 4 in 1 ability. From the right, there are four captured/ crucified ultramen on planet Golgotha. Ultraman, Sophie, Seven, and Jack. In the 2.0 Second Impact Scene we only see 4 crosses standing. Also, in Ultraman A. There are 4 crosses on Planet Golgotha corresponding to the 4 Ultramen. If we add Ultraman Ace, we get 5 crosses. (See image below) The Crosses If you look deeply at the top of the Ultraman crosses you can see the inscription of their shorthand names (see 2.0 Q Preview magic square image). They’re freehand symbols There are only two times the 5 crosses are shown in Shin. 1.When Gendo and Fuyutsuki drive the black moon/ Nerv HQ to antartica 2.When the Wunder invades Antarctica If you look closely at the tops of these crosses in the same place, the ultraman symbols are inscribed at the top. They’re only shown for an instant so you have to look really close [looking forward to amazon release! ] It can’t be confirmed but the ultramen could be the Adams. We see the repeated ultraman motifs in the second impact flashback scene in 2.0 with the Ultraman “Color Timers” However, the second impact scene is drawn slightly different and the ultraman symbols don’t appear In Q, Mari refers to Eva 13 as the surviving Adam and there’s a possibility it may be identifiable. The Preview’s “Magic Square” We know the four symbols on the magic square in the Q preview from 2.0 correspond to Ultraman characters. The top right is Jack, the lower left is Seven, the top right is Ultraman, and the upper left is Ace. Upon checking, we can see that Sophie’s symbol is missing. If the leaning cross in Antarctica has Sophie’s symbol on it, it’s a possibility that the leaning cross is the surviving Adam aka Unit 13. If the leaning cross has another Ultraman symbol, the conclusion is the same for a different ultraman. The Rei’s in the “Magic Square”/Timeline of the creation of Wunder/Adam's Vessels Of course we know that we see Kaoru in a commander’s uniform in the preview from 2.0. He’s in the magic square with the 4 Rei’s We might look at the scene differently if the 4 Rei’s in the preview are the advanced Ayanami series. In Q, Mari tells Rei Q to get out of the Eva before she becomes an Adam’s Vessel. Moreover, at this time Gendo was not at Nerv HQ so the Busse (the Wunder) and the Adam’s Vessels were made without Gendo by Seele. This is supported by the scene when Rei gets locked out from using Unit 09 and the Wunder gets locked out of its controls at the end of Q. We can see the Seele symbols on their display screens. Even more mysteries There’s a scene in the Q preview with Mari and the Adam’s Vessels candidates (e.g. the 4 Rei’s). What is the connection between Mari and the Adam’s. What was her private conference in the preview? When Kaoru is shown in the magic square, the four Reis are called Seele’s children. Why? In Shin, Shinji calls Unit 13 Kaoru’s Eva. When Unit 13 reawakens, Kaoru’s body is made to pilot Unit 13 (from a Yamashita illustration). Is there a connection between Kaoru and the fifth adam? Perhaps we might be able to find more information when the complete records for Q and shin are released. What are your thoughts?

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Mon Jul 12, 2021 9:34 pm Thanks for this

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by hui43210 » Mon Jul 12, 2021 9:45 pm Thanks very much Pluto! While it's unfortunate this part the story hasn't been told in full, I rather like that it allows the audience to search for clues and come to their own conclusions. Also, I've come to realize that Ultraman holds the key to most of Anno's works lol.

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by Konja7 » Mon Jul 12, 2021 9:51 pm Rei Q is the sixth Rei of the advanced Ayanami series. If the other 4 Rei in that Preview are also part of that series, that would mean Rei II isn't part of the advanced Ayanami series. Also, there is fifth Rei of the advanced Ayanami series that we have never seen.

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Mon Jul 12, 2021 10:14 pm Isn't Rei Q (VI) from an early lot of Ayanami types? Asuka said something along these lines at the end of Q.

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Jul 12, 2021 11:33 pm BernardoCairo wrote: Isn't Rei Q (VI) from an early lot of Ayanami types? Asuka said something along these lines at the end of Q.

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by Pluto » Mon Jul 19, 2021 5:33 am Update Special thanks goes out to EG Founder Reichu who has been so kind to add more relevant information as well as a link to her original topic and analysis on the topic (see below). Definitely a great find and observation! tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com')) tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com')) Relevant Thread and Analysis Replies hui43210 wrote: Also, I've come to realize that Ultraman holds the key to most of Anno's works lol.

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jul 20, 2021 3:55 am Pluto wrote: Konja7#926603 wrote: Rei Q is the sixth Rei of the advanced Ayanami series. Also, there is fifth Rei of the advanced Ayanami series that we have never seen.

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Jul 20, 2021 6:25 am Imagine that Rei shows up from the Sea & this time remembers everything like she greets Hikari as "class rep" but also knows who the baby is & whatever sweater Rei did during the timeskip but she's a teetsy bit awkward around Mari cause she also remembers being nommed four times over & Mari would just be like "oops, my bad! But it was an emergency with final impact going down & all"

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by Konja7 » Tue Jul 20, 2021 7:00 am ElMariachi wrote: It's never confirmed if Rei Q come from the same batch as the four Advanced Series piloting the Adam's Vessels, Asuka's words at the end of Q suggests that she comes from the same series as Rei II, and we know in Shin that Rei Q's "official" designation is "Ayanami-Type Series Number 6" (Rei 6 for short), so the four Advanced can't be Rei III, IV, V and VI, since the 6 is already taken. (unless they actually are Rei 7, 8, 9 and 10)

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by roblucci01 » Fri Jul 23, 2021 4:31 am It's all too juicy for them to not make another film. Also, Shinji's inner psychological monologues while stuck in Unit-01 would make for a great addition. This was severely lacking in NTE, and it's disappointing because this is a cornerstone of Evangelion.

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by Jinroh » Fri Jul 23, 2021 4:17 pm roblucci01 wrote: It's all too juicy for them to not make another film.

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jul 23, 2021 7:24 pm ^ Agreed, Anno has been very clear that he's done with Evangelion, and indeed from the NHK documentary it's pretty clear that he did Shin more because he had to rather than because he wanted to do it, so if something animated comes about the proto-Q or the events of the timeskip, it'll be made from the rest of the Khara staff, or by some other studio to whom they'll lend their franchise. Or as you said, it could also be on another media, like a manga, LN or videogame. Maybe that'll be an extra chapter of Evangelion Battlefields ? roblucci01 wrote: Also, Shinji's inner psychological monologues while stuck in Unit-01 would make for a great addition. This was severely lacking in NTE, and it's disappointing because this is a cornerstone of Evangelion.

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by roblucci01 » Fri Jul 23, 2021 7:51 pm Jinroh wrote: He probably won't bother with Evangelion ever again. At best we'll have a manga or a book...

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Jul 24, 2021 5:49 am ^ This time he has been very empathetic on how done he was with the franchise, especially because of how much it hurts him to do, it's been years that he said that he wanted the franchise to go to other people's hands, and the second half of Shin is a giant meta farewell from Anno to Evangelion and how Tokusatsu is his new passion. But now that I think about it, it's possible that one day Khara (or another studio) animates the events of the timeskip or expands on the post-3I world, since Rebuild was mostly Shinji's story, that he's the character where Anno put the most of him and that most of the other characters mainly revolve around him, that actually gives more liberty to other authors to take those characters and develop them since Shinji is completely absent during the timeskip. Now the question is if such a project will interest many people, since this post-3I and its stakes completely disappear by the time of Shin's end. (hard to have tension about protecting the means to restore the world and keep hope for mankind when you know that Shinji will make all that irrelevant by repairing the world and resurrecting everyone killed by Third Impact)

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sat Jul 24, 2021 6:26 am ElMariachi wrote: But now that I think about it, it's possible that one day Khara (or another studio) animates the events of the timeskip or expands on the post-3I world, since Rebuild was mostly Shinji's story, that he's the character where Anno put the most of him and that most of the other characters mainly revolve around him, that actually gives more liberty to other authors to take those characters and develop them since Shinji is completely absent during the timeskip. Now the question is if such a project will interest many people, since this post-3I and its stakes completely disappear by the time of Shin's end. (hard to have tension about protecting the means to restore the world and keep hope for mankind when you know that Shinji will make all that irrelevant by repairing the world and resurrecting everyone killed by Third Impact)

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Jul 24, 2021 7:15 am ^ That's not wrong, the events of the proto-Q is what seems to picks up the people's interest more and the last time the characters of both sides (NERV/WILLE vs neo-NERV/SEELE) interacted until Shin, I think that's in part because we had several crumbs about it and the revelation that a movie about them was initially planned. Meanwhile the events of the war (if there really was a "war" seeing the absurd gap in firepower between both factions) are a nearly complete blank slate, which means more artistic freedom, but would also make it a harder challenge, IIRC the only thing we know happened was Misato giving birth to Kaji Jr (and that's at the beginning of the war), Asuka and Kensuke starting their friendship/parental/something relationship (which could had happened any time during the 14 years, but IIRC Tsurumaki hinted that it started very early), Toji's allusion that he had to do some very shady shit for his family's sake and of course his romantic relationship with Hikari. IMHO, a hypothetical author will either keep the close to the know cast, for which I can see a series of short mangas, a novel or a videogame, as there won't be enough material to make a whole movie of series; or they'll expand on the post-3I world, which will require to introduce new characters and settings, which might be enough for a full movie or series.

[]: Re: 2.0's Q preview and its connections to Shin             Quote Post by Pluto » Fri Aug 13, 2021 5:15 am Update Reichu has found and located the screenshots of the Ultraman symnbols on the crosses in Shin ! Thank you!! tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com'))
